page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core and single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng scores reaching 86418.33 and 2696.17 respectively. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is highly efficient at 4066.4 GB/sec, though uncached large block read and write speeds perform in the average tier at 132.04 GB/sec and 59.49 GB/sec. The processor demonstrates strong capabilities in cryptography, physics simulations, and database operations, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 25379.15 and Redis SET operations executing at 61.66 million operations per second.

The instance offers a high-density compute profile that is qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the 1.0 GB of RAM per core represents a clear tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. It is highly suited for compute-heavy workloads such as ray tracing, image processing, Redis caching layers, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference, while being less optimal for large-scale in-memory databases or workloads requiring local NVMe storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 32 physical cores and a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per core, this server is designed for high-compute workloads without local storage or GPU acceleration. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance, high-speed cached memory bandwidth, and excellent database operations, including Redis SET throughput of 61.66 million operations per second. The instance represents a qualitative balance of high compute density and cost efficiency, though the low memory-to-core ratio is a tradeoff for memory-heavy tasks. It is best suited for compute-bound workloads, image processing, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 is a compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 64 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, optimized for high-density compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs for demanding parallel processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
64.0 GB of system memory with 1.0 GB RAM per core ratio
Strong multi-core CPU and cryptography benchmark performance
High-throughput Redis performance with 61.66 million operations per second
Optimized for ray tracing, image processing, and compute-bound tasks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
